security-audit: Scans your code for security vulnerabilities — eval/exec, path traversal, hardcoded secrets, injection risks.
code-quality: Reviews your code for style issues, likely bugs, and structural problems in one pass.
smart-test: Finds untested code and generates pytest tests with edge cases and error paths.
fix-test: Diagnoses failing tests, classifies the root cause, and applies targeted fixes automatically.
bug-predict: Predicts where bugs are most likely based on code patterns, complexity, and change frequency.
doc-gen: Generates documentation from your source code — docstrings, README sections, API references.
planning: Helps you plan features, architecture, and TDD strategy before writing code.
refactor-plan: Analyzes code structure and produces a prioritized refactoring roadmap.
release-prep: Runs pre-release checks — health, security, changelog, and go/no-go assessment.
spec: Walks you from idea to working code through brainstorm, plan, review, approve, and execute phases.
attune-hub: Your starting point — discovers what you need and routes you to the right skill.
coach: Explains any Attune topic with progressive depth — concept, then how-to, then full reference.
workflow-orchestration: Runs multiple analysis workflows in sequence and combines the results.
memory-and-context: Stores and retrieves persistent context across sessions — notes, preferences, project state.
dependency-management: What to check when adding or updating dependencies — security, compatibility, licensing, pinning.
error-handling-design: How to design error handling — when to catch vs propagate, exception hierarchies, logging patterns.
configuration-setup: How to set up project configuration — env vars, config files, secrets management, validation.
debugging-sessions: Systematic debugging strategies — breakpoints, tracing, binary search, profiling, common gotchas.
api-endpoint-design: How to design REST endpoints — validation, error responses, authentication, pagination, versioning.
database-migrations: How to change your database schema safely — Alembic, rollbacks, zero-downtime, data migrations.
git-workflow: Branch strategies, merge conflict resolution, rebase, cherry-pick, stash, and recovery techniques.
logging-observability: How to set up structured logging — log levels, correlation IDs, PII scrubbing, rotation.
authentication-patterns: How to add authentication — JWT, sessions, OAuth2, password hashing, CSRF protection.
package-publishing: How to publish a Python package — version bump, build, TestPyPI, production publish, verify.
ci-cd-pipeline: How to set up CI/CD — GitHub Actions, matrix testing, caching, security gates, deployment.
code-migration: How to plan code migrations — Python version upgrades, framework swaps, codemods, compatibility.
